words.def: in a way that shows you have little hope and are ready to do anything without worrying about danger to yourself or others
words.example: She looked desperately around for a weapon.

words.def: the act of destroying something; the process of being destroyed
words.example: the destruction of the rainforests

words.def: a small individual fact or item; a less important fact or item
words.example: an expedition planned down to the last detail

words.def: giving many details and a lot of information; paying great attention to details
words.example: a detailed description of the events

words.def: to discover or notice something, especially something that is not easy to see, hear, etc.
words.example: The tests are designed to detect the disease early.

words.def: the quality that makes you continue trying to do something even when this is difficult
words.example: fierce/grim/dogged determination

words.def: having made a definite decision to do something and not letting anyone prevent you
words.example: The opposition to her plan made her more determined than ever.

words.def: to be different from somebody/something
words.example: They hold differing views.

words.def: to make a hole in the ground or to move soil from one place to another using your hands, a tool or a machine
words.example: I think I'll do some digging in the garden.

words.def: a physical or mental condition that makes it difficult for somebody to do some things that most other people can do
words.example: a physical/developmental/intellectual disability

words.def: having a condition that makes it difficult for you to do some things that most other people can do
words.example: physically/developmentally/intellectually disabled

words.def: a situation where people have different opinions about something and often argue
words.example: disagreement (about/on/over/as to something), Disagreement arose about exactly how to plan the show.

words.def: to make somebody feel sad because something that they hope for or expect to happen does not happen or is not as good as they hoped
words.example: disappoint (somebody), Her decision to cancel the concert is bound to disappoint her fans.

words.def: the feeling of being sad because something has not happened or been as good, successful, etc. as you expected or hoped
words.example: Book early for the show to avoid disappointment.

words.def: a thin flat round object
words.example: He wears an identity disc around his neck.

words.def: the practice of training people to obey rules and orders and punishing them if they do not; the controlled behaviour or situation that is the result of this training
words.example: The school has a reputation for high standards of discipline.

words.def: an amount of money that is taken off the usual cost of something
words.example: to get/offer a discount

words.def: to try to prevent something or to prevent somebody from doing something, especially by making it difficult to do or by showing that you do not approve of it
words.example: discourage (doing) something, a campaign to discourage smoking among teenagers

words.def: not honest; intending to trick people
words.example: Beware of dishonest traders in the tourist areas.

words.def: a thin flat round object
words.example: Red blood cells are roughly the shape of a disk.

words.def: to decide that somebody/something is not important and not worth thinking or talking about
words.example: dismiss somebody/something, to dismiss a suggestion/a claim/an idea

words.def: a condition or illness that causes problems with the way part of the body or brain works
words.example: a blood/bowel disorder

words.def: an arrangement of things in a public place to inform or entertain people or advertise something for sale
words.example: a beautiful floral display

words.def: far away in space or time
words.example: the distant sound of music

words.def: easily or clearly heard, seen, felt, etc.
words.example: There was a distinct smell of gas.

words.def: to recognize the difference between two people or things
words.example: distinguish between A and B, At what age are children able to distinguish between right and wrong?

words.def: to take somebody’s attention away from what they are trying to do
words.example: You're distracting me from my work.

words.def: to give things to a large number of people; to share something between a number of people
words.example: distribute something, The leaflets have been widely distributed.

words.def: the way that something is spread or exists over a particular area or among a particular group of people
words.example: distribution of something, They studied the geographical distribution of the disease.

words.def: an area of a country or town, especially one that has particular features
words.example: the City of London’s financial district

words.def: to interrupt somebody when they are trying to work, sleep, etc.
words.example: I'm sorry to disturb you, but can I talk to you for a moment?

words.def: a jump into deep water with your head first and your arms in front of you
words.example: a spectacular high dive (= from high above the water)

words.def: very different from each other and of various kinds
words.example: People from diverse cultures were invited to the event.

words.def: a range of many people or things that are very different from each other
words.example: the biological diversity of the rainforests

words.def: a difference between two groups of people or two things that separates them from each other
words.example: He offered advice on bridging cultural divides.

words.def: the process or result of dividing into separate parts; the process or result of dividing something or sharing it out
words.example: The organism begins as a single cell and grows by cell division.

words.def: the legal ending of a marriage
words.example: The marriage ended in divorce in 1996.

words.def: an official paper, book or electronic file that gives information about something, or that can be used as evidence or proof of something
words.example: Please read and sign the attached document.

words.def: of or inside a particular country; not foreign or international
words.example: domestic affairs/politics

words.def: more important, powerful or easy to notice than other things
words.example: The firm has achieved a dominant position in the world market.

words.def: to control or have a lot of influence over somebody/something, especially in an unpleasant way
words.example: She always says a lot in meetings, but she doesn't dominate.

words.def: to give money, food, clothes, etc. to somebody/something, especially a charity
words.example: donate something to somebody/something, He donated thousands of pounds to charity.

words.def: something that is given to a person or an organization such as a charity, in order to help them; the act of giving something in this way
words.example: a generous/large/small donation

words.def: a small round mark, especially one that is printed
words.example: There are dots above the letters i and j.

words.def: to or at a lower place or position
words.example: She jumped down off the chair.

words.def: data that is downloaded from another computer system
words.example: A staggering 99.8 % of digital music downloads are to mobile handsets.

words.def: in, towards or typical of the centre of a city, especially its main business area
words.example: a downtown store

words.def: towards the ground; towards a lower place or position
words.example: She was lying face downwards on the grass.

words.def: a group of twelve of the same thing
words.example: Give me a dozen, please.

words.def: a rough written version of something that is not yet in its final form
words.example: This is only the first draft of my speech.

words.def: to pull somebody/something along with effort and difficulty
words.example: drag somebody/something, The sack is too heavy to lift—you’ll have to drag it.

words.def: sudden, very great and often surprising
words.example: a dramatic increase/change/improvement/shift

words.def: very suddenly and to a very great and often surprising degree
words.example: Prices have fallen dramatically.
